scenario: Trump declares MOU 'largely negotiated' May 23 (CNBC, CNN, NPR); 60-day Phase 1 framework: Hormuz reopens, Iran clears mines, Iran oil sales resume, US lifts blockade + sanctions waivers; Phase 2 nuclear talks 30-60 days. Disputes: Iran's Fars claims Strait 'under Iran management'; Khamenei uranium directive (May 22) still requires uranium to stay in Iran, blocking nuclear Phase 1 concession. Brent $98.21 confirmed (TradingEconomics May 26, +0.99%); WTI $92.58 confirmed (+1.74%); TTF €46.71 confirmed (+2.47%); JKM $18.81 est. (Platts May 22 carried; Canada LNG Group May 25 weekly pending). Hormuz ~90% closed; deal not signed; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; tail 23%.

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat eases (May 26 est.; Brent $98.21 confirmed); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces extreme tail; Qafco force majeure and Cape rerouting persist until Hormuz signed + mine-cleared; fertilizer supply mechanism intact. Tail 23%. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel correlation eases with Brent $98.21 (May 26 confirmed, -6% week); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces energy premium; Cape routing disruption unchanged until Hormuz physically reopens; tail 23%.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker costs easing with Brent $98.21 (May 26 confirmed); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces tail risk; Cape routing costs persist until physical Hormuz reopening; tail 23%.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO eases with Brent $98.21 (May 26 confirmed, -6% week); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces Hormuz tail; Indonesia B40 mandate structural floor intact; Malaysia June supply gap easing on deal prospect. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure eases marginally; Brent $98.21 (May 26 confirmed); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces extreme tail; Qafco force majeure persists until deal signed + mine-cleared; planting window pressure acute near-term; tail 23%.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha falls sharply with Brent $98.21 (May 26 confirmed, -6% week); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces tail; Gulf condensate interdiction persists until Hormuz physically reopens; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Tail 23%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene tracks naphtha lower (May 26 est.; Brent $98.21 confirmed); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces price ceiling; SABIC/QAPCO shipping bottlenecks persist until mine-clearing; tail 23%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol eases on deal optimism (May 26 est.; Brent $98.21 confirmed); US Iran sanctions waiver expired Apr 19; Qatar force majeure persists until deal signed; OFAC Economic Fury (May 11) enforcement unchanged; tail 23%. China MTO complexes: feedstock pressure moderating. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea eases slightly (May 26 est.; Brent $98.21 confirmed); deal 'largely negotiated' raises prospect of Qafco fm lifting; Ras Laffan restart timeline still not before late Aug 2026 even on deal; OFAC Economic Fury (May 11) enforcement unchanged. Tail 23%. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP eases (May 26 est.; Brent $98.21 confirmed); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces tail; Saudi Ma'aden shipping disruption persists until Hormuz physically reopens; wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam acute; tail 23%.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight eases with Brent $98.21 (May 26 confirmed); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces freight premium outlook; Cape rerouting persists until Hormuz mine-cleared; palm oil plantations: input cost pressure moderating; tail 23%.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI falls on deal 'largely negotiated' (May 26 est.); Hormuz reopening prospect reduces Cape rerouting premium; P&I suspension persists until formal mine-clearing begins; physical reopening ~6 months from deal signature. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates fall sharply (May 26 est.; Brent $98.21 confirmed); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces Hormuz route-risk premium; physical reopening requires mine-clearing (~6 months); P&I restoration conditional; tail (23%) still supports elevated rate floor. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI carried (weekly; published Friday May 23). Cape rerouting premium to persist until Hormuz physically reopens + P&I restoration + mine-clearing (~6 months even on deal); deal 'largely negotiated' reduces new-high risk; tail 23%. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly).
